name: SCC second-dataset replication: CaRPool-seq (GSE213957) doubles
status: PRE-REGISTERED BEFORE downloading/reconstructing CaRPool-seq coverage. Freezes the estimand + clearing criterion + falsifiers identical to the Norman powered re-analysis (prereg 77bcaea9) so the replication bar cannot be moved after seeing the data.
motivation: merit path-to-80 requires a SECOND independent combinatorial dataset (the Norman result is one dataset). Replogle 2022 has NO doubles (all single-gene CRISPRi). CaRPool-seq is the only public laptop-scale scRNA-seq combinatorial screen with matched constituent singles: 28 singles + 158 doubles, THP1 AML, Cas13d RNA knockdown.

dataset.citation: Wessels H-H, Satija R, et al. 'Efficient combinatorial targeting of RNA transcripts in single cells with Cas13 RNA Perturb-seq.' Nature Methods 2023. PMID 36550277.
dataset.expected_composition: ~28 single-gene + ~158 double-gene perturbations, matched singles by design; ~31k cells; multimodal (3' scRNA-seq + CITE-seq).

cross_context_caveats_declared_upfront: MODALITY: CaRPool-seq is Cas13d RNA knockdown vs Norman CRISPRa overexpression -> opposite perturbation direction.
CELL TYPE: THP1 AML (differentiating myeloid) vs Norman K562 (erythroleukemia).

This is a CROSS-MODALITY, CROSS-DIRECTION replication, NOT a like-for-like reproduction. A positive result is therefore a STRONGER generalization claim; a negative is consistent with SCC being assay-specific and must be reported as such.
No public K562/CRISPRa second dataset exists (Norman is effectively unique in that niche) -> this is the best available, not a perfect match.

reconstruction.readout: TRANSCRIPTOME (RNA counts), not the ADT/surface-protein channel — to keep the target commensurate with Norman's y=||transcriptional effect||.
reconstruction.normalization: CP10K -> log1p per cell, identical to the Norman pipeline (src/run_norman_stream.py).
reconstruction.pseudobulk: mean log1p-CP10K over cells within each perturbation group (each single gene, each double pair, and NT control).
reconstruction.effect: e_g = pseudobulk(g) - pseudobulk(NT control), per gene-perturbation.
reconstruction.eligible_doubles: a double A+B is TESTABLE iff BOTH constituent singles A and B are present as single-gene groups; drop doubles missing a single.

target_and_predictor.true_mag: ||e_AB|| = L2 norm of the double's transcriptional effect vector (top-2000 highly-variable genes, selected on CONTROL cells only to avoid leakage).
target_and_predictor.additive_mag: ||e_A + e_B|| = L2 norm of the summed single effects (the biologically principled additive null).
target_and_predictor.s_hat: sum of the two single-effect magnitudes ||e_A|| + ||e_B|| (SINGLES ONLY, never the double), then standardized (z-score). IDENTICAL definition to Norman.

estimand_FROZEN: In a logistic regression of the per-pair coverage indicator (1 = true_mag in the conformal interval) on CONTINUOUS standardized s_hat, with terms [method, s_hat, method x s_hat] stacked over vanilla and SCC rows, the INTERACTION coefficient (SCC vs vanilla coverage-vs-synergy slope) is > 0 (one-sided).
coverage_generation: Leave-one-pair-out (LOPO): each double held out once; conformal calibration on the others (60/40 predictor/calib split); interval = additive_pred +/- width; width constant (vanilla) or isotonic sigma(s_hat)*Q (SCC). alpha=0.10, nominal=0.90. IDENTICAL to Norman powered re-analysis.

inference.primary: synergy-covariate PERMUTATION of the interaction statistic (B=2000), one-sided p
inference.secondary: pair-level CLUSTER BOOTSTRAP 95% CI on the interaction coefficient (B=2000)

composite_clearing_criterion_FROZEN: CLEARS within-tier iff: (c1) interaction coeff > 0 at one-sided perm p<0.05 AND (c2) vanilla high-synergy (top-50%, median split) coverage CI excludes nominal 0.90 from below AND (c3) SCC high-synergy coverage CI contains 0.90. Median (top-50%) split is frozen — NOT a tercile — matching the Norman powered prereg 77bcaea9.
REPLICATION_SUCCESS_DEFINITION: PRIMARY replication endpoint = c1 alone (interaction > 0 at perm p<0.05) reproduced on CaRPool-seq. This is the estimand that was well-powered and PASSED on Norman (p=0.0005). The full composite (c1&c2&c3) is reported but was NOT cleared on Norman either, so it is NOT the replication bar; it is reported for completeness.

tier_rule.clears_80: PRIMARY endpoint (c1) reproduces on CaRPool-seq with perm p<0.05 AND same sign -> SCC's synergy-conditioning effect is validated on TWO independent combinatorial datasets across modality/direction -> tier change to 80-83.
tier_rule.stays_high_70s: c1 does not reproduce (interaction CI spans 0, or negative sign, or perm p>=0.05) -> honest negative: SCC is Norman-specific / does not transfer across modality -> stays 77-79, reported as a clean negative.
tier_rule.honest_prior: ~45/55. The cross-modality flip (knockdown vs overexpression) is a real risk to transfer; but the additive-decomposition dynamic the effect rides on is a generic property of double perturbations, so transfer is plausible.

falsifiers: F1: interaction coeff CI spans 0 on CaRPool-seq -> does not replicate -> negative.
F2: interaction coeff is NEGATIVE (SCC worsens synergy-dependence) -> anti-replication -> strong negative.
F3: fewer than ~40 testable doubles survive reconstruction -> UNDERPOWERED, report as inconclusive (not a pass or a clean fail); pre-registered power floor.
F4: s_hat does not correlate with measured synergy on CaRPool-seq (rho ~0) -> no synergy signal to condition on -> negative by construction.
leakage_guards: s_hat from singles only (||e_A||+||e_B||), never the double's outcome
highly-variable genes selected on CONTROL cells only
LOPO removes the test double from its own calibration
permutation shuffles only the synergy covariate
no hyperparameter or split re-choice after seeing coverage
no_tuning_attestation: This prereg is hashed and committed BEFORE the CaRPool-seq matrix is downloaded. The engine (src/trustlayer/scc.py) is frozen and already validated on Norman. Only the input synergy table changes. Any deviation from this spec will be reported explicitly as a deviation.
explicit_ceiling: Even a clean c1 replication may be judged by reviewers to fall short of 80 if the cross-modality mismatch is deemed too large to count as 'the same phenomenon.' That judgement is reported honestly, not pre-empted.
